title: Null/zero-safety for every real performanceScore consumer
acceptance_criteria: performance-suggestions/route.ts: the guard that currently does `!scan.performanceScore` (which 404s a genuine score of 0) is changed to an explicit `scan.performanceScore === null || scan.performanceScore === undefined` (and equivalent for grade/metrics) so a real worst-performing site with score 0 still gets suggestions, while a true UNAVAILABLE (null) is correctly treated as no-data.

performance-section.tsx: the falsy `>=` comparison chain (the block around the current lines ~104-109 that branches on the score) is made explicit-null-safe so score 0 renders as 0 (not treated as missing) and a null/UNAVAILABLE score renders the 'not measured' state, never NaN and never a misleading grade.

Only genuinely existing consumers are touched. The previously-assumed 'better than X% of sites' percentile does NOT exist in the codebase and must NOT be invented; if a PDF/print path consuming performanceScore is found within these two files' render tree it is made null/0-safe too, otherwise it is handled by T-08's owner of the relevant file (do not modify files outside scope — report any additional consumer found).
No behavioural change for a normal numeric score (1-100); only the 0 and null/undefined edges change.
Unit/component tests cover: score 0 -> suggestions returned (no 404) and rendered as 0; score null -> 'not measured' state, no NaN; normal score unchanged. npm run lint and npm run test pass.
output_format: code plus a plain-English explanation of the 0-vs-null distinction and a list of any additional consumers discovered (for the orchestrator to route)
